finding_title: Timelock can be bypassed
source_root_cause: Governor can immediately replace governor and set delay to zero, bypassing the intended timelock process.
source_exploitation_method: Current governor assigns a new governor, sets delay to zero, then exercises privileged operations immediately.
source_fix_strategy: Require timelock self-calls for setGovernor and setDelay and administer roles through the timelock.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 263; Timelock.sol
violated_invariant: Privileged timelock operations should only execute after queued delay.
desynchronization_step: setGovernor and setDelay can be called directly by governor instead of through delayed timelock execution.
notes: Access-control/timelock-bypass bug; not labeled MV-SI because it is reducible to ordinary privileged access control.

finding_title: Unable to remove liquidity in Recovery Mode
source_root_cause: Recovery-mode whitelist is coupled to UniswapHandler, but removeLiquidity sends withdrawn Malt directly to the user, who is not whitelisted.
source_exploitation_method: When recovery mode is active, removing liquidity through the whitelisted handler still triggers Malt transfer verification from the pair to msg.sender and reverts.
source_fix_strategy: Remove liquidity to UniswapHandler first, then forward proceeds through an allowed path.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 323; README Recovery Mode docs; UniswapHandler.sol; PoolTransferVerification.sol
violated_invariant: A whitelisted recovery-mode liquidity exit path must keep the verified transfer recipient synchronized with the whitelisted handler path.
coupled_state_entities: recovery-mode transfer block, whitelist(UniswapHandler), removeLiquidity recipient msg.sender
desynchronization_step: UniswapHandler consumes whitelist status but routes the AMM withdrawal to an unwhitelisted user address.
notes: Strict re-audit: recovery-mode routing/whitelist mismatch; no protocol-maintained state constituent is updated while a stale counterpart reaches the sink.

finding_title: getAuctionCore function returns wrong values out of order
source_root_cause: AuctionEscapeHatch destructures getAuctionCore return values with one missing comma, binding active to preAuctionReserveRatio.
source_exploitation_method: earlyExitReturn and _calculateMaltRequiredForExit use the wrong returned value when deciding auction-exit behavior, risking user funds.
source_fix_strategy: Correct the tuple destructuring so active is bound to the tenth return value.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 63; AuctionEscapeHatch.sol; Auction.sol
violated_invariant: AuctionEscapeHatch must consume the active flag returned by Auction.getAuctionCore, not an adjacent return slot.
coupled_state_entities: getAuctionCore return tuple, active flag, preAuctionReserveRatio
desynchronization_step: Missing comma shifts the consumed tuple field from active to preAuctionReserveRatio.
notes: Tuple-order programming error; not treated as state synchronization.

finding_title: AuctionBurnReserveSkew.getPegDeltaFrequency wrong implementation can result in improper Liquidity Extension use
source_root_cause: Partial observation windows use auctionAverageLookback as the denominator even when only count observations exist.
source_exploitation_method: Before the observation ring is full, getPegDeltaFrequency underestimates peg delta frequency and feeds an inaccurate realBurnBudget calculation.
source_fix_strategy: Use count as the denominator while count is less than auctionAverageLookback.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 294; AuctionBurnReserveSkew.sol
violated_invariant: A moving average over observed peg deltas must divide by the number of valid observations, not by unfilled lookback capacity.
desynchronization_step: getPegDeltaFrequency consumes a fixed lookback denominator without synchronizing it to the shorter valid observation count.
notes: Strict re-audit: denominator/count bug is reducible to arithmetic/formula error, not MV-SI.

finding_title: AuctionEschapeHatch.sol#exitEarly updates state of the auction wrongly
source_root_cause: exitEarly subtracts penalty-adjusted maltQuantity but full amount commitment, corrupting the userMaltPurchased/userCommitment ratio.
source_exploitation_method: A user splits profitable early exits so the ratio rises after each exit, reducing penalties and leaving commitment that can later claim arbitrage.
source_fix_strategy: Redesign auction participation accounting so all proportional fields are updated consistently.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 268; AuctionEscapeHatch.sol; Auction.sol
violated_invariant: A participant's remaining commitment and remaining purchased Malt must decrease proportionally when an early exit consumes auction position.
desynchronization_step: exitEarly/amendAccountParticipation updates commitment by full amount while updating Malt purchased by a penalty-adjusted amount.
notes: Clear multi-variable accounting desynchronization.

finding_title: TIMELOCK_ROLE Has Absolute Power to Withdraw All FUND May Raise Red Flags for Investors
source_root_cause: TIMELOCK_ROLE can emergency-withdraw all funds from protocol contracts.
source_exploitation_method: A timelock-role holder can route funds to arbitrary destinations through emergency withdrawal functions.
source_fix_strategy: Disclose or constrain emergency withdrawal powers, preferably via pause-only controls or hardcoded/multisig destinations.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 125; Permissions.sol
violated_invariant: Emergency controls should not allow unilateral arbitrary fund withdrawal beyond disclosed trust assumptions.
desynchronization_step: Privileged emergency withdrawal consumes role authority to transfer all funds.
notes: GitHub issue carried a duplicate label, but the final C4 report and judge note explicitly kept it distinct from H-01.

finding_title: Frontrunning in UniswapHandler calls to UniswapV2Router
source_root_cause: UniswapHandler passes zero minimum output for swaps and liquidity removal.
source_exploitation_method: Mempool attackers sandwich guaranteed trades to extract value from protocol calls using UniswapHandler.
source_fix_strategy: Add slippage bounds for swaps and removeLiquidity operations.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 219; UniswapHandler.sol
violated_invariant: Protocol swaps should enforce minimum received amounts against adverse price movement.
coupled_state_entities: amountOutMin, AMM reserves, UniswapHandler swap inputs
desynchronization_step: swap and removeLiquidity use zero minimums, allowing sandwich execution at manipulated prices.
notes: MEV/slippage bug rather than stale coupled state.

finding_title: AbstractRewardMine.sol#setRewardToken is dangerous
source_root_cause: Changing rewardToken resets reward accounting expectations while accumulated stake padding remains from the old token context.
source_exploitation_method: Admin changes rewardToken; totalDeclaredReward may become zero while _userStakePadding and _globalStakePadding still affect reward share calculations.
source_fix_strategy: Remove setRewardToken or make reward token immutable; otherwise migrate/reset accounting consistently.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 285; AbstractRewardMine.sol; README reward docs
violated_invariant: Reward token identity and accumulated stake padding must refer to the same reward accounting domain.
desynchronization_step: setRewardToken updates token identity without synchronizing accumulated padding and declared-reward state.
notes: Privileged path, but the vulnerability is the unsynchronized reward-accounting state.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for setRewardToken/reward mine accounting.

finding_title: The Power Structure is Too Centralized And Protocol May Break If Anything Happen to Admin
source_root_cause: Protocol roles are heavily admin-managed, creating operational and malicious-admin risk.
source_exploitation_method: If the admin is unavailable or malicious, many protocol roles and functions can break or be abused.
source_fix_strategy: Transfer administration to multisig or DAO and reduce unnecessary privileged roles.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 124; role-managed contracts
violated_invariant: Protocol operation should not depend on a single admin for broad role management.
desynchronization_step: Centralized admin controls role configuration across many contracts.
notes: Trust/centralization finding, not a state-desynchronization bug.

finding_title: _notSameBlock() can be circumvented in bondToAccount()
source_root_cause: The same-block guard tracks msg.sender, but bondToAccount can be called through different intermediary contracts.
source_exploitation_method: An attacker uses different forwarding contracts so each call has a different msg.sender and bypasses the intended same-block guard.
source_fix_strategy: Add access control to bondToAccount or redesign the guard around the protected logical actor/action.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 195; Bonding.sol; Permissions.sol
violated_invariant: Same-block action restrictions should bind to the intended logical actor, not only the immediate caller when forwarding is allowed.
desynchronization_step: bondToAccount consumes per-sender guard state while allowing the sender identity to be changed through forwarding contracts.
notes: Guard-bypass/reentrancy-pattern issue; not labeled MV-SI.

finding_title: AbstractRewardMine - Re-entrancy attack during withdrawal
source_root_cause: _withdraw transfers reward tokens before updating _userWithdrawn and _globalWithdrawn.
source_exploitation_method: A token with transfer hooks re-enters withdrawal while earned still observes old withdrawn state and drains rewards.
source_fix_strategy: Apply checks-effects-interactions by updating withdrawn accounting before transfer.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 333; AbstractRewardMine.sol
violated_invariant: Withdrawn accounting must be advanced before external token transfer can re-enter reward withdrawal.
desynchronization_step: _withdraw performs external transfer before synchronizing withdrawn accounting.
notes: Reentrancy-rooted issue; MV-SI litmus excludes bugs reducible to reentrancy.

finding_title: MovingAverage.setSampleMemory may break exchangeRate in StabilizerNode.stabilize
source_root_cause: setSampleMemory changes sampleMemory without properly repositioning samples, counter, and active sample state.
source_exploitation_method: After an admin changes sampleMemory, getValueWithLookback can read mispositioned samples, return near-zero or revert, and distort stabilize exchangeRate.
source_fix_strategy: Remove setSampleMemory or fully migrate ring-buffer indexes and active samples.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 313; MovingAverage.sol; StabilizerNode.sol
violated_invariant: Moving-average sample capacity, active samples, and counter position must describe the same ring-buffer history.
desynchronization_step: setSampleMemory updates capacity without synchronizing ring-buffer position and active sample history.
notes: Admin-triggered but semantic defect is stale moving-average buffer state.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for sampleMemory/exchangeRate desynchronization.

finding_title: _getFirstSample returns wrong sample if count < sampleMemory
source_root_cause: _getFirstSample assumes the samples ring buffer has wrapped and returns counter+1 even when early slots are still valid.
source_exploitation_method: Before the buffer fills, TWAP computation reads a zero-initialized sample and returns an incorrect average.
source_fix_strategy: Return index 0 when counter+1 is still less than sampleMemory, or otherwise track valid sample count.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 252; MovingAverage.sol
violated_invariant: The first sample used for TWAP must be synchronized with whether the ring buffer has wrapped.
coupled_state_entities: counter, sampleMemory, samples, valid sample count
desynchronization_step: _getFirstSample consumes counter/sampleMemory without checking that the buffer has wrapped or all samples are valid.
notes: Strict MV/SV re-audit: read-side ring-buffer index/formula bug. It reads the wrong sample before wraparound, but the source does not establish an execution path that updates one state constituent while leaving a required counterpart stale; not SV-SI either because it is not same-variable stale state.

finding_title: UniswapHandler.maltMarketPrice returns wrong decimals
source_root_cause: maltMarketPrice computes price in rewardDecimals but returns maltDecimals in one branch.
source_exploitation_method: Callers may scale the returned price incorrectly if they rely on the decimals return value.
source_fix_strategy: Return rewardDecimals for the rewardDecimals < maltDecimals branch.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 255; UniswapHandler.sol
violated_invariant: A price value and its returned decimals metadata must use the same unit scale.
desynchronization_step: maltMarketPrice returns a decimals counterpart that does not match the computed price scale.
notes: Unit metadata mismatch; not a persistent state synchronization issue.

finding_title: AuctionParticipant.sol setReplenishingIndex mistake could freeze unclaimed tokens
source_root_cause: Admin can set replenishingIndex too high and cannot lower it, skipping auctions with unclaimed tokens.
source_exploitation_method: A mistaken or malicious index increase strands claimable auction tokens below the new replenishingIndex.
source_fix_strategy: Remove or constrain the setter; advance one index at a time only after proving prior auctions are fully claimed.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 88; AuctionParticipant.sol
violated_invariant: replenishingIndex must not advance past auctionIds that still have claimable participant rewards.
desynchronization_step: setReplenishingIndex updates the cursor without synchronizing against claimable-token state for earlier auctions.
notes: Cursor/reward-state desynchronization.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for replenishingIndex/unclaimed-token freeze.

finding_title: No max for advanceIncentive
source_root_cause: DAO admin can set advanceIncentive to an arbitrarily large value.
source_exploitation_method: advance then mints an excessive incentive amount to callers, enabling dilution or rug-pull behavior.
source_fix_strategy: Enforce a reasonable maximum advanceIncentive.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 190; DAO.sol
violated_invariant: Mint incentives should be bounded by protocol economics.
desynchronization_step: setAdvanceIncentive sets an unbounded value later consumed by advance.
notes: Parameter-bound/admin-privilege bug.

finding_title: Permissions - return values not checked when sending ETH
source_root_cause: Permissions uses low-level call for ETH transfer without checking success.
source_exploitation_method: Emergency ETH withdrawal can appear successful while the destination call failed.
source_fix_strategy: Check the bool return value and revert on failed calls.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 329; Permissions.sol
violated_invariant: Emergency ETH withdrawal state should reflect whether ETH transfer actually succeeded.
desynchronization_step: emergencyWithdrawGAS and partialWithdrawGAS ignore call return values.
notes: Unchecked-call bug.

finding_title: Reducing the epoch length results in leaking value from advancement incentives
source_root_cause: DAO computes epoch eligibility from genesis timestamp and current epochLength, with no checkpoint for previous epoch lengths.
source_exploitation_method: After admin reduces epochLength, advance can be called repeatedly to mint unnecessary incentives for epochs the old schedule already covered.
source_fix_strategy: Store last epoch-length modification time or checkpoint epoch schedule when changing epochLength.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 4; DAO.sol
violated_invariant: Epoch number, epoch length, and elapsed-time checkpoint must describe the same epoch schedule before minting advance incentives.
desynchronization_step: setEpochLength updates epochLength without synchronizing the schedule baseline used by advance.
notes: Temporal checkpoint desynchronization.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for epoch-length/advancement incentive leakage.

finding_title: Wrong permissions on reassignGlobalAdmin
source_root_cause: reassignGlobalAdmin requires TIMELOCK_ROLE but revokeRole also requires the caller to have ADMIN_ROLE.
source_exploitation_method: If the timelock only has TIMELOCK_ROLE, global admin reassignment reverts and role migration can be locked out.
source_fix_strategy: Align role admin requirements with the intended caller or ensure timelock has the required admin role.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 250; Permissions.sol
violated_invariant: The role authorized to call reassignGlobalAdmin must also be authorized to perform the underlying role revocations.
desynchronization_step: Function gates on TIMELOCK_ROLE while internal AccessControl operations require ADMIN_ROLE.
notes: Access-control role configuration bug.

finding_title: Bonding does not work with fee-on transfer tokens
source_root_cause: Bonding assumes amount transferred equals amount received for stake tokens.
source_exploitation_method: A fee-on-transfer stake token makes _balanceCheck fail because the contract receives less than amount.
source_fix_strategy: Measure balance before and after transfer and use the actual received amount.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 251; Bonding.sol
violated_invariant: Bonded accounting should use actual tokens received when token transfer semantics can reduce receipts.
coupled_state_entities: requested amount, actual received balance, bonded amount
desynchronization_step: _bond consumes requested amount as if it matched balance delta.
notes: ERC20 integration/fee-on-transfer compatibility issue.

finding_title: theft of system profit
source_root_cause: Above-peg stabilization can be sandwiched so an attacker extracts value that would otherwise become system profit.
source_exploitation_method: Attacker sells Malt to move price, calls stabilize to push price lower, then buys back cheaper and repays flash-loaned Malt.
source_fix_strategy: Consider stabilization during above-peg sells or redesign to reduce sandwichable profit extraction.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 56; PoolTransferVerification.sol; StabilizerNode.sol
violated_invariant: Above-peg system-profit capture should not be externally sandwichable at LP/user expense.
coupled_state_entities: Malt AMM price, stabilize trade, system profit
desynchronization_step: Attacker manipulates AMM price around stabilize execution.
notes: MEV/price-manipulation issue; not labeled MV-SI.

finding_title: Auction collateralToken will not work if token is fee-on-transfer token
source_root_cause: Auction passes realCommitment to LiquidityExtension even if a fee-on-transfer collateral token delivers less than that amount.
source_exploitation_method: purchaseAndBurn reverts because LiquidityExtension balance is below the amount parameter after transfer fees.
source_fix_strategy: Use before/after balance deltas and pass actual received collateral to purchaseAndBurn.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 227; Auction.sol; LiquidityExtension.sol
violated_invariant: Auction commitment accounting should use actual collateral received when transfer semantics reduce receipts.
coupled_state_entities: realCommitment, LiquidityExtension collateral balance, purchaseAndBurn amount
desynchronization_step: purchaseArbitrageTokens forwards nominal commitment without synchronizing to the LiquidityExtension balance delta.
notes: ERC20 integration/fee-on-transfer compatibility issue.

finding_title: AuctionParticipant.sol purchaseArbitrageTokens should not push duplicate auctions
source_root_cause: AuctionParticipant appends currentAuction to auctionIds on every purchase, allowing duplicate auction IDs.
source_exploitation_method: Duplicate IDs cause claim/replenishingIndex logic to encounter repeated auctions with no additional yield, breaking incentive claims without manual intervention.
source_fix_strategy: Prevent duplicate auction IDs, e.g. by using a mapping or membership check.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 87; AuctionParticipant.sol
violated_invariant: auctionIds and replenishingIndex must represent each auction with claimable participant state exactly once.
desynchronization_step: purchaseArbitrageTokens updates auctionIds without synchronizing against existing membership for the current auction.
notes: Duplicate index/cursor desynchronization.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for duplicate auction participation state.

finding_title: MiningService.setBonding should use BONDING role instead of REINVESTOR one
source_root_cause: setBonding swaps REINVESTOR_ROLE instead of BONDING_ROLE.
source_exploitation_method: After changing bonding, BONDING_ROLE remains unmanaged or assigned incorrectly, breaking intended role access.
source_fix_strategy: Swap BONDING_ROLE in setBonding.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 370; MiningService.sol
violated_invariant: The bonding address setter must update the bonding role, not an unrelated reinvestor role.
desynchronization_step: setBonding updates bonding but synchronizes the wrong role constant.
notes: Wrong-role configuration bug.

finding_title: Users Can Contribute To An Auction Without Directly Committing Collateral Tokens
source_root_cause: purchaseArbitrageTokens separates realCommitment accounting from collateral already sent to LiquidityExtension.
source_exploitation_method: A user directly sends collateral to LiquidityExtension, calls purchaseArbitrageTokens with amount zero, and receives purchased Malt effects without commitment cap accounting.
source_fix_strategy: Require nonzero realCommitment and base auction accounting on actual committed collateral flow.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 188; Auction.sol; LiquidityExtension.sol
violated_invariant: Auction purchased Malt, commitment caps, and participant commitments must be synchronized to the same collateral contribution.
coupled_state_entities: realCommitment, auction.commitments, LiquidityExtension collateral balance, maltPurchased
desynchronization_step: Direct token transfer updates LiquidityExtension balance without synchronizing auction commitment accounting.
notes: Strict MV/SV re-audit: retained as MV-SI based on the accepted report framing: external collateral balance/purchase result is consumed without synchronizing auction commitment/cap accounting. Local code path is worth later adjudication during matching, but label is from known-finding evidence.

finding_title: StabilizerNode Will Mint An Incentive For Triggering An Auction Even If An Auction Exists Already
source_root_cause: StabilizerNode and Auction use auctionActive and auctionExists inconsistently.
source_exploitation_method: When an inactive auction exists, stabilize rewards the caller even though triggerAuction returns without starting a new auction.
source_fix_strategy: Use auctionExists and auctionActive consistently before minting the auction-trigger incentive.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 191; StabilizerNode.sol; Auction.sol
violated_invariant: Auction-trigger incentives should be minted only when auction existence and active-state checks agree that a new auction was actually triggered.
coupled_state_entities: auctionActive, auctionExists, currentAuction, advance incentive mint
desynchronization_step: stabilize checks active state but triggerAuction consumes existence state and returns without synchronizing reward decision.
notes: Strict MV/SV re-audit: inconsistent auction lifecycle predicates/reward-before-effect bug. Valid known bug, but better explained as an effect check mismatch than stale coupled-state reaching the mint sink; not SV-SI.

finding_title: _calculateMaltRequiredForExit Uses Spot Price To Calculate Malt Quantity In exitEarly
source_root_cause: AuctionEscapeHatch uses manipulable spot price from Uniswap reserves to calculate early-exit Malt quantity.
source_exploitation_method: An attacker manipulates spot price with a flash loan and exits auction position on favorable manipulated terms.
source_fix_strategy: Use a TWAP or manipulation-resistant oracle for exit pricing.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 215; AuctionEscapeHatch.sol; UniswapHandler.sol
violated_invariant: Auction early-exit pricing should use manipulation-resistant price data.
coupled_state_entities: spot price, auction exit amount, AMM reserves
desynchronization_step: _calculateMaltRequiredForExit reads spot reserve price directly for an economic payout calculation.
notes: Oracle/spot-price manipulation issue rather than update omission.

finding_title: addLiquidity Does Not Reset Approval If Not All Tokens Were Added To Liquidity Pool
source_root_cause: UniswapHandler approves full balances before addLiquidity but does not reset unused approval when router consumes less than the full balance.
source_exploitation_method: Dust approvals accumulate and can cause token compatibility issues or leave stale router allowance.
source_fix_strategy: Reset approval when maltUsed or rewardUsed is less than approved balance.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 228; UniswapHandler.sol
violated_invariant: Router allowance should be synchronized to the actual token amount consumed by addLiquidity.
coupled_state_entities: malt approval, reward approval, maltUsed, rewardUsed, token balances
desynchronization_step: addLiquidity consumes less than approved amount without synchronizing residual approvals to zero.
notes: SV-SI under corrected definition: stale residual ERC20 allowance remains after addLiquidity partially consumes approved tokens; single state entity is the router allowance, and the sink is later token approval/transfer compatibility or external-call behavior.

finding_title: _distributeRewards Does Not Reset Approval If Not All Tokens Were Allocated
source_root_cause: StabilizerNode approves Auction for a fixed reward amount before allocateArbRewards, but Auction may allocate less.
source_exploitation_method: Residual token approval accumulates in StabilizerNode and can cause stale allowance or token compatibility issues.
source_fix_strategy: Reset approval when allocated reward is less than the approved rewarded amount.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 229; StabilizerNode.sol; Auction.sol
violated_invariant: Auction reward allowance should equal the amount actually allocated, with no stale residual approval.
coupled_state_entities: rewarded approval, allocateArbRewards consumed amount, StabilizerNode allowance
desynchronization_step: _distributeRewards approves a fixed amount and fails to synchronize allowance after partial allocation.
notes: SV-SI under corrected definition: stale residual rewardToken allowance remains after allocateArbRewards consumes less than the approved amount; single state entity is the Auction allowance, and the sink is later token approval/transfer compatibility or external-call behavior.

finding_title: AMM pool can be drained using a flashloan and calling stabilize
source_root_cause: StabilizerNode gates stabilization on short TWAP but calculates trade size from directly manipulated AMM reserves.
source_exploitation_method: Attacker waits for TWAP threshold, flashloan-manipulates pool reserves, calls stabilize, and causes excessive rewardToken removal/distribution.
source_fix_strategy: Use TWAP or manipulation-resistant data for trade-size calculation.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 372; StabilizerNode.sol; UniswapHandler.sol
violated_invariant: The stabilization trigger price and trade-size price must be synchronized to manipulation-resistant views of the same pool state.
coupled_state_entities: TWAP exchangeRate, spot AMM reserves, trade size, rewardToken pool balance
desynchronization_step: stabilize checks TWAP but consumes unsynchronized spot reserves for _calculateTradeSize.
notes: Strict re-audit: spot-price manipulation/oracle-design issue; not an update omission leaving a stale counterpart.

finding_title: Dutch auction can be manipulated
source_root_cause: Auction finalization only occurs when commitments reach maxCommitments or later stabilize paths run.
source_exploitation_method: Attacker buys all but one wei of commitment, preventing automatic finalization while auction price continues to fall, then gets a reliable discount.
source_fix_strategy: Incentivize or expose safe finalization, or lock in buyer price at commitment time.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 375; Auction.sol; StabilizerNode.sol
violated_invariant: Auction commitments, max commitment threshold, and finalization/price state must stay synchronized when the auction is effectively filled.
coupled_state_entities: auction.commitments, auction.maxCommitments, auctionEndTime, auction price, active flag
external_proxy_state: AMM rounding behavior for tiny swaps
desynchronization_step: purchaseArbitrageTokens leaves commitments just below max, so finalization state is not synchronized with economically filled auction state.
notes: Strict re-audit: auction rounding/finalization design issue; not a stale coupled-state update omission.

finding_title: Slippage checks when adding liquidity are too strict
source_root_cause: UniswapHandler.addLiquidity uses entire contract balances and 95 percent minimums, assuming local balances match pool ratio.
source_exploitation_method: An attacker sends tokens directly to UniswapHandler to unbalance balances, causing addLiquidity to revert.
source_fix_strategy: Avoid direct-balance assumptions; accept caller parameters or compute balanced amounts from reserves.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 257; UniswapHandler.sol
violated_invariant: Local token balances consumed by addLiquidity must be synchronized with the AMM reserve ratio used for minimum amounts.
coupled_state_entities: maltBalance, rewardBalance, AMM reserve ratio, amountAMin, amountBMin
desynchronization_step: Direct token transfer updates local balances without synchronizing the ratio assumptions used by addLiquidity min amounts.
notes: Strict re-audit: direct balance griefing/slippage assumption issue; not a protocol-maintained stale counterpart.

finding_title: Bonding.sol _unbondAndBreak does not account for edge case where no tokens are returned
source_root_cause: UniswapHandler returns LP tokens to Bonding when removeLiquidity returns zero Malt or reward tokens, but Bonding expects token proceeds.
source_exploitation_method: User unbonds and breaks LP during the zero-return edge case; LP tokens are sent back to Bonding and become stuck instead of returned to user.
source_fix_strategy: Handle the zero-return case in Bonding by reverting or forwarding LP tokens back to the user.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 234; Bonding.sol; UniswapHandler.sol
violated_invariant: Bonding._unbondAndBreak return handling must be synchronized with all possible UniswapHandler removeLiquidity return modes.
coupled_state_entities: LP tokens, amountMalt, amountReward, Bonding user claim
desynchronization_step: UniswapHandler sends LP tokens back but Bonding only synchronizes Malt/reward return paths.
notes: Strict re-audit: cross-contract return-mode edge case; not a multi-variable stale-state issue under the strict litmus.

finding_title: User can bypass Recovery Mode via UniswapHandler to buy Malt
source_root_cause: Recovery mode blocks buying Malt, but UniswapHandler is whitelisted and addLiquidity can be abused with directly supplied tokens.
source_exploitation_method: User swaps DAI to Malt with recipient UniswapHandler, adds matching liquidity, and gains exposure despite the buy block.
source_fix_strategy: Disable addLiquidity or otherwise constrain handler paths during recovery mode.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 325; README Recovery Mode docs; UniswapHandler.sol; PoolTransferVerification.sol
violated_invariant: Recovery-mode buy restrictions and whitelisted handler liquidity paths must enforce the same Malt-acquisition policy.
coupled_state_entities: recovery-mode buy block, whitelist(UniswapHandler), UniswapHandler balances, addLiquidity
desynchronization_step: Direct swap to whitelisted handler updates handler balances without synchronizing the recovery-mode policy consumed by addLiquidity.
notes: Strict re-audit: recovery-mode whitelist/path bypass; no required counterpart state is left stale by an update path.

finding_title: Malt Protocol Uses Stale Results From MaltDataLab Which Can Be Abused By Users
source_root_cause: MaltDataLab price and pool moving averages are updated by an external updater rather than inline with state-changing protocol paths.
source_exploitation_method: If updates are censored or delayed, stabilize and other sensitive calls consume stale MaltDataLab values and can mint repeated incentives or misprice protocol actions.
source_fix_strategy: Inline MaltDataLab tracking updates where relevant and use cumulative/TWAP data rather than spot samples.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 59.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 373; README MaltDataLab docs; MaltDataLab.sol; StabilizerNode.sol
violated_invariant: Protocol actions that depend on MaltDataLab price/reserve snapshots must synchronize those snapshots with current pool state before economic decisions.
coupled_state_entities: maltPriceAverage, pool reserve averages, trackMaltPrice, trackPoolReserves, stabilize exchangeRate
desynchronization_step: Sensitive paths consume MaltDataLab snapshots without synchronizing them to recent pool-state changes.
notes: Canonical stale external oracle/snapshot MV-SI candidate.

finding_title: ERC20 transferFrom return values not checked
source_root_cause: ERC20 transferFrom return values are ignored in payment paths.
source_exploitation_method: A token can return false without reverting, allowing NFT mint/payment logic to proceed despite failed payment.
source_fix_strategy: Check transferFrom success or use SafeERC20.safeTransferFrom.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 52; ERC721Payable.sol; CoreCollection.sol
violated_invariant: Payment transfer success should be required before mint/payment effects are accepted.
desynchronization_step: Payment path consumes unchecked transferFrom result and continues execution.
notes: Unchecked ERC20 transferFrom return value. Payment failure can be ignored, but this is not a persistent multi-state desynchronization invariant.

finding_title: Splitter: Anyone can call incrementWindow to steal the tokens in the contract
source_root_cause: incrementWindow trusts caller-provided RoyaltyVault interface responses instead of a specific authorized vault.
source_exploitation_method: A fake RoyaltyVault can increment windows so balanceForWindow exceeds real splitter funds, then claims drain contract tokens.
source_fix_strategy: Restrict incrementWindow to the authorized RoyaltyVault address.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 3; Splitter.sol
violated_invariant: Splitter window accounting should correspond to real funds received from the authorized royalty vault.
coupled_state_entities: balanceForWindow, currentWindow, Splitter token balance, authorized RoyaltyVault
desynchronization_step: Unauthorized caller advances accounting windows without corresponding authorized funds.
notes: Unauthorized incrementWindow can corrupt splitter window accounting relative to actual funds, but root cause is missing access control/fake vault access, so do not count as MV-SI.

finding_title: DoS: claimForAllWindows() May Be Made Unusable By An Attacker
source_root_cause: claimForAllWindows iterates over unbounded currentWindow.
source_exploitation_method: An attacker or organic growth can make currentWindow large enough that claimForAllWindows exceeds block gas limits.
source_fix_strategy: Add ranged claims over startWindow/endWindow.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 6; Splitter.sol
violated_invariant: Claiming should remain feasible as the number of splitter windows grows.
desynchronization_step: claimForAllWindows loops over every window.
notes: Unbounded-loop / gas DoS in claimForAllWindows, not stale state.

finding_title: CoreCollection can be reinitialized
source_root_cause: CoreCollection.initialize sets initialized but does not enforce it.
source_exploitation_method: Owner can rerun initialize and reset core collection variables such as payableToken.
source_fix_strategy: Add onlyUnInitialized guard to initialize.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 4; CoreCollection.sol
violated_invariant: CoreCollection initialization should be one-time lifecycle setup.
desynchronization_step: initialize can be called repeatedly by owner.
notes: Reinitialization / lifecycle access-control bug, not MV/SV state inconsistency.

finding_title: Centralisation Risk: Owner Of RoyaltyVault Can Take All Funds
source_root_cause: RoyaltyVault owner can set platformFee arbitrarily high.
source_exploitation_method: Owner can set platform fee to capture all vault funds or block sendToSplitter/NFT transfer flows.
source_fix_strategy: Cap platformFee, prevent increases, and/or send current funds to splitter before fee changes.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 9; RoyaltyVault.sol
violated_invariant: Royalty fee controls should not allow a privileged owner to take or block all funds.
desynchronization_step: Owner changes platformFee to abusive value.
notes: Centralized owner can change/take fees. This is privileged-power risk, not strict MV-SI.

finding_title: STORAGE COLLISION BETWEEN PROXY AND IMPLEMENTATION (LACK EIP 1967)
source_root_cause: Proxy stores implementation/admin state in ordinary storage layout rather than EIP-1967 slots.
source_exploitation_method: Implementation storage changes can collide with proxy storage and brick or corrupt the contract.
source_fix_strategy: Use EIP-1967 unstructured storage slots or standard proxy implementations.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 108; CoreProxy.sol
violated_invariant: Proxy and implementation storage layouts must not alias security-critical slots.
coupled_state_entities: proxy storage slots, implementation storage slots
desynchronization_step: Upgrade/use of implementation aliases storage slots.
notes: Proxy/implementation storage collision. Real state inconsistency through storage aliasing, but not MV-SI/SV-SI synchronization failure.

finding_title: Duplicate NFTs Can Be Minted if payableToken Has a Callback Attached to it
source_root_cause: mintToken performs payableToken payment before mint effects update totalSupply.
source_exploitation_method: A callback-enabled payableToken reenters mintToken while totalSupply is stale, bypassing supply/tokenId assumptions and minting duplicate NFTs.
source_fix_strategy: Add reentrancy protection and follow checks-effects-interactions.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 121; CoreCollection.sol; ERC721Payable.sol
violated_invariant: The tokenId/supply calculation must consume an up-to-date totalSupply during mint.
desynchronization_step: External token callback reenters before totalSupply advances for the in-progress mint.
notes: Reentrant stale totalSupply() before mint state updates, allowing duplicate token IDs. One stale state entity, not multi-variable.

finding_title: Funds cannot be withdrawn in CoreCollection.withdraw
source_root_cause: withdraw uses transferFrom(address(this), msg.sender, amount) instead of transfer.
source_exploitation_method: ERC20 allowance requirements can make the collection unable to withdraw its own funds.
source_fix_strategy: Use transfer for self-held tokens or SafeERC20.safeTransfer.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 80; CoreCollection.sol
violated_invariant: CoreCollection should use the correct ERC20 method to move its own token balance.
desynchronization_step: withdraw calls transferFrom without self-allowance.
notes: Wrong ERC20 method: transferFrom instead of transfer; compatibility/API misuse, not stale state.

finding_title: ERC20 tokens with no return value will fail to transfer
source_root_cause: RoyaltyVault uses ERC20 interface calls that expect boolean return data.
source_exploitation_method: Non-standard ERC20 tokens with no return value can revert despite successful transfer semantics.
source_fix_strategy: Use SafeERC20.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 83; RoyaltyVault.sol
violated_invariant: Token transfer wrappers should handle non-standard ERC20 return behavior.
desynchronization_step: transfer call expects return data that may not exist.
notes: ERC20 no-return compatibility issue, not stale or coupled state.

finding_title: DoS: Attacker May Front-Run createSplit() With A merkleRoot Causing Future Transactions With The Same merkleRoot to Revert
source_root_cause: createSplit uses merkleRoot as CREATE2 salt.
source_exploitation_method: Attacker front-runs with the same merkleRoot/salt so the legitimate split deployment reverts.
source_fix_strategy: Remove salt or include msg.sender/project-specific data in salt.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 33; SplitFactory.sol
violated_invariant: Split deployment salt should not be front-runnable by unrelated users.
desynchronization_step: Front-runner consumes the same CREATE2 salt before legitimate transaction.
notes: CREATE2/merkleRoot front-running DoS, not state desynchronization.

finding_title: Fixed Amount of Gas Sent in Call May Be Insufficient
source_root_cause: attemptETHTransfer uses a fixed 30000 gas stipend.
source_exploitation_method: Receiver contracts or future gas repricing may make ETH withdrawals fail.
source_fix_strategy: Avoid fixed gas stipend and use reentrancy guards / CEI.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 8; Splitter.sol
violated_invariant: ETH transfer path should remain compatible with receiver gas needs.
desynchronization_step: attemptETHTransfer forwards fixed gas to receiver.
notes: Fixed gas stipend issue, not state inconsistency.

finding_title: RoyaltyVault.sol is Not Equipped to Handle On-Chain Royalties From Secondary Sales
source_root_cause: Protocol lacks on-chain secondary-sale royalty handling despite docs/intent.
source_exploitation_method: Secondary royalties depend on off-chain marketplace configuration and can be bypassed on other marketplaces.
source_fix_strategy: Implement on-chain royalty support such as ERC2981.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 130; README.md; RoyaltyVault.sol
violated_invariant: Royalty vault should support intended secondary-sale royalty flows.
desynchronization_step: Secondary-sale royalties are not implemented on-chain.
notes: Missing on-chain royalty support; missing functionality rather than stale coupled state.

finding_title: createProject can be frontrun
source_root_cause: Anyone can call createProject for an available project id.
source_exploitation_method: Attacker front-runs project creation and becomes collection owner.
source_fix_strategy: Use whitelist or signed authorization for project creation.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 26; CoreFactory.sol
violated_invariant: Project ownership should bind to the intended creator, not first mempool caller.
desynchronization_step: Attacker consumes available project id before intended user.
notes: Project creation front-running / ownership capture.

finding_title: Gas costs will likely result in any fees sent to the Splitter being economically unviable to recover
source_root_cause: Each NFT sale can create a separate splitter window, forcing costly per-window claims.
source_exploitation_method: Royalty recipients may spend more gas claiming small per-window fees than the fees are worth.
source_fix_strategy: Remove or redesign window mechanism; make royalty splitting asynchronous/aggregated.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 27; CoreCollection.sol; RoyaltyVault.sol; Splitter.sol
violated_invariant: Royalty claim design should not make normal fees economically unrecoverable.
desynchronization_step: Window-per-sale design forces many small claims.
notes: Gas/economic impracticality from per-window claims, not stale state.

finding_title: CoreCollection's token transfer can be disabled
source_root_cause: Royalty splitting during token transfer assumes royaltyAsset handles zero transfers and direct tiny balances safely.
source_exploitation_method: Attacker manipulates RoyaltyVault balance to one wei so sendToSplitter attempts zero/invalid transfers and blocks NFT transfers for certain tokens.
source_fix_strategy: Make sendToSplitter fail-safe, add thresholds, or avoid requiring royalty distribution during NFT transfers.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 37; CoreCollection.sol; RoyaltyVault.sol; Splitter.sol
violated_invariant: NFT transfers should not depend on fragile immediate royalty split execution for arbitrary ERC20s.
desynchronization_step: Direct tiny transfer manipulates balance assumptions and makes transfer hook revert.
notes: Token compatibility / transfer-disable DoS through zero-transfer behavior and direct-balance assumptions, not strict MV-SI.

finding_title: Ineffective Handling of FoT or Rebasing Tokens
source_root_cause: RoyaltyVault records nominal splitterShare in Splitter windows rather than actual tokens received after transfer.
source_exploitation_method: FoT/rebasing tokens make Splitter balance diverge from recorded window amount, causing overclaims, underclaims, or stuck funds.
source_fix_strategy: Measure Splitter balance before/after transfer and increment window by actual received amount; document unsupported tokens if not supported.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 43; RoyaltyVault.sol; Splitter.sol
violated_invariant: Splitter recorded window amount must equal actual Splitter token balance increase for that window.
desynchronization_step: sendToSplitter transfers nominal splitterShare then incrementWindow records the nominal amount without synchronizing to actual received balance.
notes: Real multi-state accounting desync: nominal splitterShare / recorded window amount can diverge from actual Splitter token balance for FoT/rebasing tokens.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for Splitter nominal-share/actual-balance FoT/rebasing accounting.

finding_title: CoreCollection: Starting index is pseudo-randomly generated, allowing for gameable NFT launches
source_root_cause: startingIndex randomness uses predictable block.number-derived data.
source_exploitation_method: Users can predict future starting indexes and game NFT minting to target rare items.
source_fix_strategy: Use stronger randomness such as commit-reveal or VRF-like source.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 50; CoreCollection.sol
violated_invariant: NFT starting index should be unpredictable before mint ordering decisions.
desynchronization_step: Predictable entropy is consumed to set startingIndex.
notes: Weak randomness / gameable starting index.

finding_title: Differing percentage denominators causes confusion and potentially brick claims
source_root_cause: Code defines PERCENTAGE_SCALE differently from denominator actually used in calculations.
source_exploitation_method: Users may build Merkle splits with the wrong denominator, causing claim amounts to exceed funds and fail.
source_fix_strategy: Remove unused PERCENTAGE_SCALE or align denominator constants and docs.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 53; Splitter.sol
violated_invariant: Percentage denominator used in split proofs should match the contract's calculation denominator.
desynchronization_step: Incorrect denominator is used to compute claim amounts.
notes: Percentage denominator/formula mismatch; not update desynchronization.

finding_title: Add a timelock to setPlatformFee()
source_root_cause: Critical platform fee changes lack timelock delay.
source_exploitation_method: Users cannot react before privileged fee changes take effect.
source_fix_strategy: Add timelock to setPlatformFee.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 112; SplitFactory.sol; RoyaltyVault.sol
violated_invariant: Critical governance fee changes should be delayed.
desynchronization_step: Owner changes platformFee immediately.
notes: Missing timelock / governance-risk recommendation.

finding_title: Not handling return value of transferFrom command can create inconsistency
source_root_cause: transferFrom return value is unchecked before emitting events.
source_exploitation_method: If transferFrom fails by returning false, protocol emits events that downstream systems may treat as successful transfer/payment.
source_fix_strategy: Require transferFrom success before emitting success events.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 81; CoreCollection.sol; ERC721Payable.sol
violated_invariant: Events should reflect actual successful token transfer state.
desynchronization_step: Function emits event after unchecked transferFrom failure.
notes: False event emission after unchecked transferFrom; event/log inconsistency, not protocol storage MV/SV state.

finding_title: CoreCollection.setRoyaltyVault doesn't check royaltyVault.royaltyAsset against payableToken, resulting in potential permanent lock of payableTokens in RoyaltyVault
source_root_cause: CoreCollection can bind to a RoyaltyVault whose royaltyAsset differs from the collection payableToken.
source_exploitation_method: Minting fees in payableToken are transferred to an incompatible RoyaltyVault that only distributes royaltyAsset, permanently locking payableToken funds.
source_fix_strategy: Check payableToken equals RoyaltyVault.royaltyAsset before setting royaltyVault.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 104.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 73; CoreCollection.sol; ERC721Payable.sol; RoyaltyVault.sol
violated_invariant: CoreCollection.payableToken must equal RoyaltyVault.royaltyAsset for fee routing to be withdrawable/distributable.
desynchronization_step: setRoyaltyVault binds a vault without synchronizing/checking its royaltyAsset against payableToken; _handlePayment later sends payableToken to that vault.
notes: Cross-contract configuration-state invariant: CoreCollection.payableToken must equal RoyaltyVault.royaltyAsset; mismatch can permanently lock minting fees.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for payableToken/royaltyAsset configuration mismatch.

finding_title: Wrong shortfall calculation
source_root_cause: OptimisticLedger.settleAccount double-adds existing shortfall when computing a new shortfall.
source_exploitation_method: Settling an account with negative balance inflates global shortfall beyond the actual deficit, blocking correct withdrawals unless funds are donated.
source_fix_strategy: Assign the computed shortfall once instead of adding the previous shortfall twice.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 60.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 18; OptimisticLedger.sol
violated_invariant: Global shortfall should equal the sum of unresolved account deficits.
desynchronization_step: settleAccount computes shortfall as existing shortfall plus new deficit, then adds that computed value back into self.shortfall.
notes: Arithmetic/accounting formula error. It does not require stale or desynchronized multi-state entities.

finding_title: withdrawTo Does Not Sync Before Checking A Position's Margin Requirements
source_root_cause: Collateral.withdrawTo performs collateral withdrawal without settling the account/product first, so margin checks can use stale oracle-derived position maintenance.
source_exploitation_method: A user can withdraw collateral while an account is undercollateralized if the oracle/product state has not been synced before the maintenance invariant check.
source_fix_strategy: Call settleForAccount before withdrawal margin checks so maintenance is evaluated against current oracle-settled position state.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 60.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 74; Collateral.sol; AccountPosition.sol
violated_invariant: Before collateral withdrawal, account collateral must remain sufficient for the current settled position maintenance computed from the latest oracle/product version.
coupled_state_entities: Collateral ledger balance, account position maintenance state, oracle/product price version
counterpart_state_entity: product account position maintenance/current and next maintenance
external_proxy_state: oracle price/version used by product.maintenance and product.maintenanceNext
desynchronization_step: withdrawTo debits collateral and then checks maintenanceInvariant without first settling/syncing the account to the latest oracle/product version.
notes: Strict MV-SI: concrete collateral/position/oracle-price synchronization invariant; stale maintenance reaches the collateral withdrawal/accounting decision.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for withdrawTo missing account/product settlement before maintenance checks.

finding_title: No checks if given product is created by the factory
source_root_cause: Collateral accepts arbitrary product addresses instead of checking that a product was created by the protocol factory.
source_exploitation_method: A malicious product can return arbitrary maintenance values, trap deposits, and later liquidate or claim collateral from users who interact with the fake product.
source_fix_strategy: Verify product addresses against a factory-maintained registry or query the factory before accepting product interactions.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 60.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 63; Collateral.sol
violated_invariant: Collateral interactions should only accept products created by the trusted factory.
desynchronization_step: Collateral methods trust an arbitrary product address supplied by the caller.
notes: Missing product validation / trust-boundary bug, not stale state or strict MV-SI.

finding_title: Multiple initialization of Collateral contract
source_root_cause: Collateral.initialize lacks a one-time initializer guard, allowing setup values to be changed through repeated initialization under specific deployment conditions.
source_exploitation_method: A malicious deployer or setup flow can initialize with one token/factory configuration, perform actions, and later reinitialize with the intended configuration, hiding the earlier setup.
source_fix_strategy: Add a proper initializer guard / UInitializable pattern so Collateral can only be initialized once.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 60.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 13; Collateral.initialize
violated_invariant: Collateral initialization should be one-time and immutable after deployment setup.
desynchronization_step: initialize can be called multiple times when the first setup uses a zero factory path.
notes: Initialization/lifecycle control risk, not strict MV/SV stale-state inconsistency.

finding_title: Chainlink's latestRoundData might return stale or incorrect results
source_root_cause: ChainlinkOracle.sync reads latestRoundData without validating stale rounds, round completion, or nonzero/positive price conditions.
source_exploitation_method: The oracle can record or use stale Chainlink data as the protocol price, feeding stale price into downstream margin/accounting decisions.
source_fix_strategy: Validate Chainlink round data using answeredInRound, timestamp, round completion, and positive price checks before accepting the value.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 60.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 24; ChainlinkOracle.sol
violated_invariant: Accepted oracle price should be fresh and valid before being stored or used as the current protocol price.
desynchronization_step: sync accepts latestRoundData without stale-round validation, allowing a stale price value to become the protocol oracle value.
notes: SV-SI: stale oracle read / missing Chainlink stale-data validation; not MV-SI because no persistent multi-entity synchronization invariant is required.

finding_title: OpenLevV1Lib's and LPool's doTransferOut functions call native payable.transfer, which can be unusable for smart contract calls
source_root_cause: Native-token withdrawals use payable.transfer with the fixed 2300 gas stipend.
source_exploitation_method: Smart-contract recipients with fallback logic can be unable to receive native-token withdrawals, causing closeTrade, liquidate, redeem, borrow, or repay flows to fail.
source_fix_strategy: Replace transfer with checked low-level call or Address.sendValue in the affected native-token transfer paths.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 72.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 75; OpenLevV1Lib.sol; LPool.sol
violated_invariant: Native-token transfer paths should support smart-contract recipients that need more than the transfer gas stipend.
desynchronization_step: doTransferOut uses payable.transfer, so some recipient contracts cannot receive funds.
notes: Gas-stipend / transfer-compatibility bug. It freezes withdrawals for some smart-contract recipients, but there is no multi-state synchronization invariant.

finding_title: UniV2ClassDex.sol#uniClassSell() Tokens with fee on transfer are not fully supported
source_root_cause: uniClassSell checks computed buyAmount against minBuyAmount instead of the actual bought balance delta for fee-on-transfer tokens.
source_exploitation_method: A fee-on-transfer buy token can make the actual amount received lower than minBuyAmount while the computed getAmountOut value still passes the slippage check.
source_fix_strategy: Compare the actual bought amount from before/after balance delta against minBuyAmount.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 72.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 208; UniV2ClassDex.sol
violated_invariant: Slippage validation should be based on the actual amount received, not only the computed swap estimate.
coupled_state_entities: computed buyAmount and actual bought balance delta
external_proxy_state: Uniswap-style pair reserves and fee-on-transfer token behavior
desynchronization_step: uniClassSell validates buyAmount before computing and checking the actual bought delta.
notes: Slippage/fee-on-transfer validation bug. It uses a computed amount instead of actual bought amount, not a persistent protocol state desynchronization.

finding_title: Missing payable
source_root_cause: LPool.addReserves uses msg.value but is not marked payable.
source_exploitation_method: Users cannot add native-token reserves through a function that reads msg.value because calls with ETH revert at the ABI/modifier boundary.
source_fix_strategy: Mark addReserves payable when it is intended to accept native ETH value.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 72.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 61; LPool.sol
violated_invariant: Functions that consume msg.value for native reserves must be payable.
desynchronization_step: addReserves reads msg.value but the function is not payable.
notes: ABI/function-modifier bug: function uses msg.value but is not payable. No stale state or coupled state.

finding_title: Eth sent to Timelock will be locked in current implementation
source_root_cause: Governor/Timelock execution routes proposal ETH value from msg.sender rather than from the Timelock balance.
source_exploitation_method: ETH held by Timelock cannot be sent out by governance execution; successful value transfer requires the executor to provide fresh ETH.
source_fix_strategy: Execute queued transactions through Timelock without forwarding user-supplied msg.value as the source of proposal funds.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 72.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 80; GovernorAlpha.sol; Timelock.sol
violated_invariant: Governance execution should route ETH value from the Timelock-held funds when executing proposals.
coupled_state_entities: Timelock ETH balance and proposal execution value
desynchronization_step: execute sends proposal value from msg.sender instead of using ETH already held by Timelock.
notes: Governance execution/value-routing bug. The proposal sends ETH from msg.sender, not the Timelock balance. No MV-SI invariant.

finding_title: OpenLevV1.closeTrade with V3 DEX doesn't correctly accounts fee on transfer tokens for repayments
source_root_cause: The V3 closeTrade sell path trusts the DEX-returned receiveAmount for repayment accounting instead of measuring the actual token balance received after fee-on-transfer behavior.
source_exploitation_method: A taxed token can make actual funds received lower than the DEX-indicated receive amount, while debt repayment accounting consumes the larger reported amount, creating protocol deficit or failed closeTrade transfers.
source_fix_strategy: Use before/after balance checks for V3 DEX sell logic and base repayment/user-return accounting on actual received tokens.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 72.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 104; OpenLevV1.sol; UniV3Dex.sol
violated_invariant: Debt repayment accounting during closeTrade must be synchronized with the actual token amount received by the contract after the swap.
coupled_state_entities: actual OpenLevV1 token balance received, DEX-reported receiveAmount, internal repayment/debt accounting
primary_state_entity: actual contract/token balance received after swap
counterpart_state_entity: internal repayment/debt accounting consumed by closeTrade
external_proxy_state: V3 DEX-reported receiveAmount and fee-on-transfer token behavior
desynchronization_step: closeTrade / V3 sell path consumes the DEX-returned receiveAmount for repayment without synchronizing it to the actual before/after token balance.
notes: Clear MV-SI: actual funds received can be less than DEX-reported receive amount, but the reported amount is consumed for debt repayment accounting.

finding_title: anti-flashloan mechanism may lead to protocol default
source_root_cause: The anti-flashloan price guard can revert liquidation when spot price is more than the allowed difference below TWAP during fast downward markets.
source_exploitation_method: Liquidations can remain blocked while prices trend down, allowing undercollateralized positions to persist and potentially creating protocol default.
source_fix_strategy: Use TWAP to determine health or redesign the liquidation guard so legitimate liquidations are not blocked during rapid market moves.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 72.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 233; OpenLevV1Lib.sol
violated_invariant: Liquidation eligibility checks should not block necessary liquidations during legitimate fast downward markets.
coupled_state_entities: spot price and TWAP liquidation guard
external_proxy_state: AMM spot price and TWAP price
desynchronization_step: The maxLiquidationPriceDiffientRatio guard can revert liquidation when spot remains far below TWAP.
notes: Liquidation-design / price-check bug. The spot-vs-TWAP guard can block liquidations during fast downward markets, but it is not a missed synchronization of coupled state entities.

finding_title: User can forfeit other user rewards
source_root_cause: ExtraRewardsDistributor.getReward allows a third party to provide a high _startIndex for another account and advance that account's reward claim cursor.
source_exploitation_method: An attacker calls getReward for a victim with _startIndex above the victim's last claim index, causing skipped epochs to be treated as claimed.
source_fix_strategy: Do not allow arbitrary users to claim for other accounts, or constrain _startIndex against the account's own claim cursor.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 50; ExtraRewardsDistributor.sol
violated_invariant: A user's reward claim cursor should only advance over epochs whose rewards are actually paid to that user.
desynchronization_step: getReward accepts _account and _startIndex, then writes userClaims for that account past unclaimed epochs.
notes: Single reward checkpoint/index issue: userClaims[_token][user] can be advanced by a third party using _startIndex, causing skipped rewards.

finding_title: BaseRewardPool4626 is not IERC4626 compliant
source_root_cause: BaseRewardPool4626 omits or misimplements required ERC-4626 interface behavior.
source_exploitation_method: Integrators expecting ERC-4626-compliant vault semantics can fail to integrate correctly with BaseRewardPool4626.
source_fix_strategy: Remove the contract or implement the ERC-4626-required functions and events.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 26; BaseRewardPool4626.sol
violated_invariant: ERC-4626 wrappers should comply with the ERC-4626 interface contract.
desynchronization_step: Contract exposes a non-compliant ERC-4626 surface.
notes: ERC-4626 interface noncompliance. No stale/coupled state invariant.

finding_title: CrvDepositorWrapper.sol relies on oracle that isn't frequently updated
source_root_cause: CrvDepositorWrapper relies on a Balancer TWAP oracle from a low-activity pool that can go long periods without updating.
source_exploitation_method: Infrequently updated TWAP data can make output assumptions stale, causing slippage failures or sandwichable distribution behavior.
source_fix_strategy: Use fresher Chainlink-style pricing or have the keeper provide appropriate minOut values rather than relying on the stale TWAP guideline.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 115; CrvDepositorWrapper.sol
violated_invariant: Oracle price used for output/slippage decisions should be fresh enough to reflect current asset values.
desynchronization_step: CrvDepositorWrapper consumes a TWAP that may not have updated for over an hour.
notes: Stale/infrequently updated oracle price problem. Single external oracle state, not MV-SI.

finding_title: Improperly Skewed Governance Mechanism
source_root_cause: AuraLocker total voting supply tracks all locked balances while individual voting power only tracks delegated balances.
source_exploitation_method: Undelegated locked balances inflate total voting supply without corresponding individual voting power, making governance percentage thresholds difficult or impossible to satisfy.
source_fix_strategy: Track total voting supply using delegated balances only, or otherwise synchronize total supply with the individual voting-power accounting model.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 232; AuraLocker.sol
violated_invariant: Total voting supply should correspond to the sum of effective individual voting power under the same delegation model.
coupled_state_entities: total voting supply checkpoint, individual delegated voting-power checkpoints, locked balances
desynchronization_step: Locking contributes to total supply, but individual votes only advance through delegated-balance checkpointing.
notes: Clear governance MV-SI: circulating voting supply and actual individual voting power are desynchronized.

finding_title: AuraLocker kick reward only takes last locked amount into consideration, instead of whole balance
source_root_cause: AuraLocker kick reward formula uses the last lock amount instead of the user's whole locked balance in the all-expired-locks path.
source_exploitation_method: Kickers receive less reward than intended, reducing incentive to process expired locks.
source_fix_strategy: Use the total locked balance in the approximate kick reward calculation for the all-expired path.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 156; AuraLocker.sol
violated_invariant: Kick reward approximation should be based on the intended eligible locked balance.
desynchronization_step: _processExpiredLocks calculates reward from locks[length - 1].amount rather than locked.
notes: Reward formula/approximation bug, not a desynchronization path.

finding_title: Users can grief reward distribution
source_root_cause: ExtraRewardsDistributor rejects backdated reward epochs if a later epoch has already been added.
source_exploitation_method: An attacker can front-run with a dust reward in a later epoch, making a legitimate reward addition for an earlier epoch revert.
source_fix_strategy: Allow safe backdating or otherwise handle reward epoch insertion without dust front-running grief.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 180; ExtraRewardsDistributor.sol
violated_invariant: Reward epoch insertion should not be griefable by dust additions to later epochs.
desynchronization_step: Attacker inserts a dust later epoch before a legitimate earlier-epoch reward transaction.
notes: Reward-distribution griefing via dust/front-running rewardEpochs; not stale multi-state invariant.

finding_title: Rewards distribution can be delayed/never distributed on AuraLocker.sol#L848
source_root_cause: AuraLocker reward notification can reset/extend the distribution period when tiny rewards are added on top of remaining rewards.
source_exploitation_method: An attacker can repeatedly send dust rewards and restart distribution, delaying or stretching rewards that should have distributed sooner.
source_fix_strategy: Apply queueNewRewards-style protections so small top-ups do not restart a reward period unless they exceed a threshold.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 1; AuraLocker.sol; AuraStakingProxy.sol
violated_invariant: Dust top-ups should not indefinitely delay distribution of already scheduled rewards.
desynchronization_step: Dust reward notification recomputes leftover and rewardRate over a new full period.
notes: Dust reward can reset/extend reward distribution period. Economic griefing/design issue, not strict MV-SI.

finding_title: Reward may be locked forever if user doesn't claim reward for a very long time such that too many epochs have been passed
source_root_cause: Reward claiming iterates from the user's claim cursor to the end of rewardEpochs and can exceed gas limits after many epochs.
source_exploitation_method: A long-inactive user may be unable to claim rewards because the catch-up loop becomes too expensive to execute.
source_fix_strategy: Allow bounded/partial reward claiming with caller-specified end index or another pagination mechanism.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 240; ExtraRewardsDistributor.sol; AuraLocker.sol
violated_invariant: Reward claiming should remain executable even after many reward epochs.
desynchronization_step: Claim path loops through too many epochs in one transaction.
notes: Reward claim loop can become too expensive; unbounded-loop/gas-lock issue.

finding_title: Locking up AURA Token does not increase voting power of individual
source_root_cause: AuraLocker only checkpoints delegated votes during lock; if a user has no delegatee, locked AURA/vlAURA balance is not reflected in individual voting power.
source_exploitation_method: A user who locks AURA and receives vlAURA can still have zero getVotes voting power until they manually delegate, missing governance or gauge votes.
source_fix_strategy: Auto-delegate to self or checkpoint the user's own voting power when locking if no delegatee is set.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 186; AuraLocker.sol
violated_invariant: A user's locked AURA/vlAURA balance should be synchronized with the voting checkpoint state that determines getVotes.
coupled_state_entities: locked AURA/vlAURA balance, delegatee unlocks, vote checkpoints
desynchronization_step: _lock updates locked balances but skips delegate checkpointing when delegates(_account) is zero.
notes: Clear voting-power MV-SI: locked balance and voting checkpoint/delegation state are coupled and desynced.

finding_title: Reward can be vested even after endTime
source_root_cause: AuraVestedEscrow.fund lacks a time/lifecycle check preventing funding after the vesting endTime.
source_exploitation_method: Funds sent after the vesting period can be locked or fail to become useful vesting rewards.
source_fix_strategy: Require funding before the appropriate vesting cutoff, such as before endTime or before startTime depending on intended lifecycle.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 126; AuraVestedEscrow.sol
violated_invariant: Vesting funding should respect the vesting lifecycle window.
desynchronization_step: fund can be called after endTime without rejecting the deposit.
notes: Vesting can be funded after endTime. Missing time/lifecycle check, not MV-SI.

finding_title: Increase voting power by tokenizing the address that locks the token
source_root_cause: AuraLocker allows smart contracts to lock tokens, enabling wrapper contracts to tokenize locked positions and direct accumulated voting power.
source_exploitation_method: A wrapper can attract deposits, tokenize the locker address, and delegate the contract's votes to influence governance.
source_fix_strategy: Restrict or manage smart-contract lockers with an EOA check, whitelist, or blacklist policy.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 278; AuraLocker.sol
violated_invariant: Governance lock semantics should account for tokenized contract-wallet wrappers if they are disallowed by design.
coupled_state_entities: locker address type and delegation control
desynchronization_step: A smart-contract locker can aggregate locks and delegate voting power while issuing wrapper tokens.
notes: Governance design/contract-wallet restriction issue, not state desync.

finding_title: Users may lose rewards to other users if rewards are given as fee-on-transfer tokens
source_root_cause: ExtraRewardsDistributor records reward per token from the requested _amount, but fee-on-transfer tokens can make actual received balance lower.
source_exploitation_method: The first claimants can consume the actual balance while accounting promises rewards based on the larger nominal amount, causing later claims to revert or underpay.
source_fix_strategy: Measure token balance before and after transfer and use the actual received amount for reward accounting.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 176; ExtraRewardsDistributor.sol
violated_invariant: Recorded reward accounting must match the actual reward token balance received and available for later claims.
coupled_state_entities: nominal _amount, actual reward token balance received, rewardData per-token accounting
desynchronization_step: _addReward transfers tokens, then updates rewardData using _amount rather than actual received balance delta.
notes: Asset-accounting MV-SI: reward accounting uses _amount while actual received balance can be lower.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for ExtraRewardsDistributor nominal amount vs actual FoT received-balance accounting.

finding_title: User will lose funds
source_root_cause: AuraClaimZap transfers CVX from the user when depositCvxMaxAmount is positive even if the LockCvx option is false.
source_exploitation_method: A user can transfer CVX into the zap contract without the lock branch executing, leaving funds stuck in the contract.
source_fix_strategy: Only pull CVX when LockCvx is enabled, or otherwise return/handle CVX when the option is false.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 108; AuraClaimZap.sol
violated_invariant: Zap options should align token pulls with the action that consumes those pulled tokens.
desynchronization_step: claimRewards pulls CVX before checking that LockCvx is true.
notes: Missing else / option-check issue. Funds stuck, but no persistent coupled-state invariant.

finding_title: ConvexMasterChef: When _lpToken is cvx, reward calculation is incorrect
source_root_cause: ConvexMasterChef allows a pool LP token to be the same as the cvx reward token, causing reward mints/balances to inflate lpSupply used for reward calculation.
source_exploitation_method: If _lpToken equals cvx, the balance-derived lpSupply includes reward tokens and corrupts accCvxPerShare, reducing or misallocating rewards.
source_fix_strategy: Reject pools whose LP token is the reward token, or separate reward-token custody from LP supply measurement.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 151; ConvexMasterChef.sol
violated_invariant: Per-pool lpSupply used for reward math must represent staked LP tokens only, not minted reward-token balances.
coupled_state_entities: pool LP token identity, reward token identity, balance-derived lpSupply, accCvxPerShare
desynchronization_step: add allows _lpToken == cvx, so updatePool reads an inflated cvx balance as LP supply.
notes: Configuration/accounting MV-SI: _lpToken can equal reward token cvx, corrupting reward calculation.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for ConvexMasterChef lpToken==cvx reward-supply corruption.

finding_title: Integer overflow will lock all rewards in AuraLocker
source_root_cause: AuraLocker rewardPerTokenStored uses uint96 and can overflow under extreme reward-rate and low-supply conditions.
source_exploitation_method: Overflow in reward calculation can make updateReward revert and lock reward functionality until shutdown/emergency withdrawal.
source_fix_strategy: Use wider integer storage or enforce reward-rate bounds that prevent overflow.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 261; AuraLocker.sol
violated_invariant: Reward calculations should not overflow their storage type under accepted reward configurations.
desynchronization_step: _rewardPerToken can exceed uint96 and revert on to96 conversion.
notes: Integer overflow in reward calculation. Criterion 5 excludes arithmetic errors.
