finding_title: ConvexMasterChef: safeRewardTransfer can cause loss of funds
source_root_cause: ConvexMasterChef safeRewardTransfer silently sends less than the owed reward when the contract reward-token balance is undersupplied.
source_exploitation_method: A user can invoke reward transfer and receive less than accounting says they are owed, with the missing amount not reflected in accounting.
source_fix_strategy: Use normal safeTransfer or explicitly account for and preserve unpaid reward debt rather than silently underpaying.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 272; ConvexMasterChef.sol
violated_invariant: Reward payout accounting must stay synchronized with the actual reward-token balance available for transfer and unpaid reward obligations.
coupled_state_entities: actual reward token balance, pending reward amount, user reward accounting
desynchronization_step: safeRewardTransfer caps transfer to current balance but accounting treats the reward as paid.
notes: Asset-accounting MV-SI: missing reward tokens are not reflected in accounting and users can be silently underpaid.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for safeRewardTransfer silent underpayment accounting.

finding_title: DDOS in BalLiquidityProvider
source_root_cause: BalLiquidityProvider requires contract token balance to exactly equal the requested maxAmountsIn value.
source_exploitation_method: An attacker can donate one wei before the call, making the equality check fail and preventing liquidity provision.
source_fix_strategy: Remove exact-balance equality or change it to a tolerance/greater-than-or-equal check.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 285; BalLiquidityProvider.sol
violated_invariant: External token donations should not make valid liquidity provision calls revert through exact balance equality.
coupled_state_entities: contract token balance and maxAmountsIn input
desynchronization_step: Attacker front-runs with a token donation so bal != maxAmountsIn[i].
notes: Donation/front-run DoS against exact balance equality check. External-balance griefing, not persistent MV-SI.

finding_title: ConvexMasterChef's deposit and withdraw can be reentered drawing all reward funds from the contract if reward token allows for transfer flow control
source_root_cause: ConvexMasterChef transfers pending rewards before updating user.amount and user.rewardDebt in deposit and withdraw.
source_exploitation_method: A transfer-hook reward token can reenter deposit or withdraw before reward accounting is updated and repeatedly draw rewards.
source_fix_strategy: Add nonReentrant protection and update internal accounting before external reward-token transfers.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 313; ConvexMasterChef.sol
violated_invariant: User reward accounting should be advanced before any external reward-token transfer can reenter the same accounting path.
desynchronization_step: deposit/withdraw calls safeRewardTransfer before updating user.amount and rewardDebt.
notes: Reentrancy stale-accounting issue over same reward variables. Reentrancy is not MV-SI under criterion 5.

finding_title: AuraBalRewardPool charges a penalty to all users in the pool if the AuraLocker has been shut down
source_root_cause: AuraBalRewardPool's lock-reward path calls AuraLocker.lock, which reverts after AuraLocker shutdown, leaving only the penalty path.
source_exploitation_method: Users must accept a penalty after shutdown because the lock branch is unavailable.
source_fix_strategy: Do not charge the penalty when AuraLocker is shut down, or add lifecycle-aware branching for the shutdown case.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 179; AuraBalRewardPool.sol; AuraLocker.sol
violated_invariant: Reward claim options should account for AuraLocker shutdown lifecycle.
desynchronization_step: isShutdown makes the lock path revert, forcing users to use the penalty path.
notes: Missing lifecycle branching rather than a persistent coupled-state desync.

finding_title: CrvDepositor.sol Wrong implementation of the 2-week buffer for lock
source_root_cause: CrvDepositor compares a seconds-denominated difference against 2 as if it were weeks.
source_exploitation_method: The lock extension buffer can trigger after seconds/new week boundaries rather than after the intended two-week interval.
source_fix_strategy: Normalize unlock values to weeks before comparing, or compare seconds against 2 * WEEK.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 343; CrvDepositor.sol
violated_invariant: The lock-extension buffer should compare values in the same units.
desynchronization_step: _lockCurve compares a seconds value to 2 rather than two weeks.
notes: Unit error: seconds compared as if weeks. Arithmetic/unit bug.

finding_title: massUpdatePools() is susceptible to DoS with block gas limit
source_root_cause: ConvexMasterChef.massUpdatePools loops over every pool and can exceed the block gas limit if too many pools exist.
source_exploitation_method: Pool updates may become impossible through massUpdatePools, negatively affecting reward accounting and protocol operation.
source_fix_strategy: Limit loop iterations, paginate updates, or design pool updates so the contract cannot be bricked by pool count.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 197; ConvexMasterChef.sol
violated_invariant: Pool reward update operations should remain executable under expected pool counts.
desynchronization_step: massUpdatePools iterates through all pools in one transaction.
notes: massUpdatePools block-gas DoS. Even with accounting consequences, the finding itself is unbounded-loop DoS.

finding_title: ConvexMasterChef: When using add() and set(), it should always call massUpdatePools() to update all pools
source_root_cause: ConvexMasterChef add/set can change totalAllocPoint without first updating all pool reward checkpoints.
source_exploitation_method: Rewards accrued before the denominator change can be calculated using the wrong totalAllocPoint, corrupting per-pool rewards.
source_fix_strategy: Always massUpdatePools before modifying totalAllocPoint, or remove the option to skip the update.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 147; ConvexMasterChef.sol
violated_invariant: Pool reward checkpoints must be synchronized before totalAllocPoint changes because accrued rewards depend on the old allocation denominator.
desynchronization_step: add/set modify totalAllocPoint while some pools have not been updated through the previous allocation denominator.
notes: Clear reward-accounting MV-SI: totalAllocPoint changes without first updating all pools.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for totalAllocPoint update without pool checkpoint synchronization.

finding_title: Duplicate LP token could lead to incorrect reward distribution
source_root_cause: ConvexMasterChef.add does not prevent duplicate LP tokens across pools, so multiple pools can share the same balance-derived lpSupply.
source_exploitation_method: If two pools use the same LP token, updatePool reads the same contract token balance for both pools and corrupts reward distribution.
source_fix_strategy: Track LP tokens already added and reject duplicate LP token pools.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 123.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 124; ConvexMasterChef.sol
violated_invariant: Each pool's lpSupply used for reward distribution should correspond only to that pool's unique staked LP token position.
coupled_state_entities: pool LP token identities, balance-derived lpSupply, per-pool reward accounting
desynchronization_step: add allows duplicate lpToken values, so updatePool reuses the same token balance for multiple pools.
notes: Configuration/accounting MV-SI: duplicate LP token pools cause lpSupply reuse and incorrect reward distribution.
strict_match_notes: No same-finding MV-Bench row was found for duplicate LP token pool reward accounting.

finding_title: Bonding mechanism allows malicious user to DOS auctions
source_root_cause: Auction settlement grants exclusive settlement privilege to the first bonder and only allows bond burn after a 24 hour delay.
source_exploitation_method: A malicious user can immediately bond after an auction starts, refuse to settle, and repeat the process to keep auction settlement unavailable.
source_fix_strategy: Remove exclusive bonding or allow multiple bonders / direct settlement so one actor cannot hold settlement hostage.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 41.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 48; Auction.sol; Basket.sol
violated_invariant: Auction settlement should not be exclusively blockable by one malicious bonder for the burn delay.
coupled_state_entities: auction bond ownership and settlement permission
desynchronization_step: A malicious bonder obtains exclusive settlement rights and waits through the burn delay without settling.
notes: Auction griefing / DoS. Mechanism design / exclusive settlement privilege, not stale coupled state.

finding_title: Basket.sol#mint() Malfunction due to extra nonReentrant modifier
source_root_cause: Basket.mint has nonReentrant and calls mintTo, which also has nonReentrant, so the nested call reverts.
source_exploitation_method: Users cannot use mint because the reentrancy guard is entered twice in the same call path.
source_fix_strategy: Remove the redundant nonReentrant modifier from mint and keep it on mintTo.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 41.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 59; Basket.sol
violated_invariant: Mint wrapper functions should not trip their own reentrancy guard when forwarding to guarded implementation functions.
desynchronization_step: mint enters nonReentrant and then calls mintTo, which tries to enter the same guard again.
notes: Reentrancy guard composition mistake causing mint() to revert. No coupled-state invariant.

finding_title: Setting Factory.auctionDecrement to zero causes Denial of Service in Auction.settleAuction()
source_root_cause: Factory.setAuctionDecrement allows auctionDecrement to be set to zero, and Auction.settleAuction divides by it.
source_exploitation_method: If auctionDecrement is zero, settleAuction always reverts with division by zero.
source_fix_strategy: Validate auctionDecrement is nonzero in the setter.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 41.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 24; Factory.sol; Auction.sol
violated_invariant: Auction decrement configuration should be nonzero before it is used as a divisor.
desynchronization_step: Owner can set auctionDecrement to zero, later causing division by zero during settlement.
notes: Missing config validation causing division by zero. Criterion 5 excludes arithmetic/config-zero errors from MV-SI.

finding_title: Setting Factory.bondPercentDiv to zero cause Denial of Service in Auction.bondForRebalance()
source_root_cause: Factory.setBondPercentDiv allows bondPercentDiv to be set to zero, and Auction.bondForRebalance divides by it.
source_exploitation_method: If bondPercentDiv is zero, bondForRebalance always reverts with division by zero.
source_fix_strategy: Validate bondPercentDiv is nonzero and above unsafe boundary values in the setter.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 41.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 23; Factory.sol; Auction.sol
violated_invariant: Bond percentage divisor configuration should be nonzero before it is used as a divisor.
desynchronization_step: Owner can set bondPercentDiv to zero, later causing division by zero during bonding.
notes: Missing config validation / division by zero. Not MV-SI.

finding_title: Fee on transfer tokens do not work within the protocol
source_root_cause: Protocol accounting assumes requested transfer amounts equal actual token amounts received, which fails for fee-on-transfer tokens.
source_exploitation_method: Fee-on-transfer tokens transfer less than expected, so protocol-requested/accounted amounts diverge from actual ERC20 balances and accounting breaks.
source_fix_strategy: Use stored before/after token balances or actual balance deltas instead of nominal transfer amounts for amount accounting.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 41.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 78; Basket.sol
violated_invariant: Protocol-requested/accounted token amounts must stay synchronized with actual ERC20 balances held by the basket/protocol.
coupled_state_entities: actual ERC20 token balances, protocol-requested/accounted token amounts
desynchronization_step: Token transfer path records or requests nominal amounts without synchronizing against the actual balance delta.
notes: Clear asset-accounting MV-SI. Fee-on-transfer tokens transfer less than expected, breaking protocol accounting.

finding_title: createBasket re-entrancy
source_root_cause: Factory.createBasket interacts with arbitrary ERC20-like tokens in a loop without nonReentrant protection.
source_exploitation_method: A token callback hook could reenter during basket creation and observe or manipulate partially initialized state/order of operations.
source_fix_strategy: Add nonReentrant protection to createBasket and follow checks-effects-interactions where possible.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 41.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 85; Factory.sol
violated_invariant: Basket creation should not be reentered while its state/action sequence is partially complete.
desynchronization_step: createBasket calls token interactions before reentrancy protection prevents callback reentry.
notes: Reentrancy risk from token callbacks while creating a basket. Reentrancy is excluded from MV-SI, so this is SV-SI/control.

finding_title: Validations
source_root_cause: Several setters and user inputs lack validation, including zero divisors, zero bounty amounts, fee cap bounds, zero recipient, self-token inclusion, and empty metadata.
source_exploitation_method: Invalid configuration or input values can cause DoS, accidental loss, or unsafe protocol states.
source_fix_strategy: Add explicit validation checks for the listed configuration and input constraints.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 41.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 84; Factory.sol; Auction.sol; Basket.sol
violated_invariant: Protocol configuration and inputs should satisfy documented safety bounds.
desynchronization_step: Invalid values can be accepted and later trigger arithmetic, DoS, or safety failures.
notes: Bundle of missing validations: config/arithmetic/access-safety checks, not a specific MV-SI oracle row.

finding_title: Basket becomes unusable if everybody burns their shares
source_root_cause: Basket.handleFees divides by totalSupply and computes a new ibRatio even after all shares have been burned and totalSupply is zero.
source_exploitation_method: After everyone burns, the next mint triggers handleFees with totalSupply zero, reverting fee/ibRatio accounting and making the basket unusable.
source_fix_strategy: In handleFees, return or reset ibRatio to BASE when totalSupply is zero before computing fees.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 41.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 49; Basket.sol
violated_invariant: When basket totalSupply reaches zero, ibRatio and fee-accounting state must be reset or bypassed so future mint/fee accounting remains valid.
desynchronization_step: All holders burn shares, advancing totalSupply to zero without synchronizing ibRatio/fee handling for the empty basket state.
notes: Strict MV-SI: totalSupply can become zero while fee handling still tries to compute/update ibRatio; basket becomes unusable.

finding_title: Auction bonder can steal user funds if bond block is high enough
source_root_cause: Auction newRatio decays with bondBlock/time and is not bounded against the basket's current ibRatio or a safe minimum.
source_exploitation_method: A bonder can wait until newRatio falls below current ibRatio or near zero, settle, and extract too much underlying while leaving too little in the basket.
source_fix_strategy: Cap or bound newRatio during settlement, for example requiring newRatio above basket.ibRatio or a minimum ibRatio, or require bond and settle together.
evidence_source: Web3Bugs report 41.md; Code4rena report; GitHub issue 51; Auction.sol; Basket.sol
violated_invariant: Auction settlement ratio must remain synchronized with basket ibRatio and underlying balances so settlement cannot extract more assets than the basket accounting can support.
coupled_state_entities: auction-time-derived newRatio, basket ibRatio, totalSupply/pendingWeights, underlying token balances
counterpart_state_entity: basket ibRatio and underlying balance requirements
external_proxy_state: block/time decay via bondBlock and auctionStart
desynchronization_step: As bondBlock increases, newRatio decays without a minimum or synchronization check against current ibRatio before settlement consumes it.
notes: Strict MV-SI / temporal accounting: decayed newRatio can fall below current ibRatio and let settlement extract too much underlying.
